mission.title: cloudevents: Map and Object type problems
mission.description: Map and Object type problems. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current cloudevents setup
steps.description:
Verify your cloudevents version:
CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
This feature requires cloudevents as a dependency.

steps.title: Review cloudevents configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:
This is related to #184. Currently the spec introduces a lot of confusion around Maps and Objects when it comes to JSON.

So, the data attribute is an Object, which means it can be Map, String or Binary. Map is a map of name to Object pairs.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Map and Object type problems
steps.description:
The CloudEvents v1.0 specification resolved the Map and Object type confusion. Extension attribute values are now restricted to: Boolean, Integer, String, Binary, URI, URI-Reference, or Timestamp. Complex types like Map and Object should be serialized to String or Binary format before being used as extension values.

See the source issue and CloudEvents v1.0 spec for details.

steps.title: Upgrade cloudevents to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade cloudevents:
Update the cloudevents dependency in your project manifest to the latest version.
See the project's releases: https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/releases

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Map and Object type problems" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The Map and Object type problems were addressed as part of the CloudEvents v1.0 specification. The spec clarified that extension attribute values must be one of: Boolean, Integer, String, Binary, URI, URI-Reference, or Timestamp. Complex types like Map and Object are not directly supported as extension values — they should be serialized to String or Binary if needed.

prerequisites.description: CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
